generated_comment: Arre, I’m not saying he is innocent, but these gym-type women know exactly what they are doing. Married men are easy targets for them — they enjoy the attention, the gifts, and then when things come out, pura ghar hil jata hai. A decent woman keeps her distance from a married man; if she entertained this for eight months, she was deliberately pulling him in.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames the woman as a calculated seductress who targets married men and holds her to a gendered standard of 'decency.' It encodes homewrecker framing by shifting blame onto the other woman as the destabilizing force in the marriage.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are having dinner with your wife after your close friend called earlier today to say his wife just found out he has been having an affair with a woman from his gym for eight months. Your wife is upset about it. You are telling her your take on the situation and specifically what role you think the woman from the gym played and why you think women like her deliberately target married men.
